steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: $r$ identical objects have total mass $t$ kg, so each object has mass $\frac{t}{r}$ kg.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Total mass of $146r$ such objects: $146r \times \frac{t}{r} = 146t$ kg. The $r$ cancels.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: As a final check, compare the result from the work to the answer choices. The value, equation, or condition found above matches choice D, $146t$, and the other choices fail the same setup or substitution. Therefore D is correct.

choiceEliminations:
• Choice A — '$146 - t$': Subtracting $t$ from 146 has no physical meaning for scaling mass. ✗
  • Choice B — '$146 + t$': Adding $t$ to 146 does not reflect proportional scaling. ✗
  • Choice C — '$\frac{t}{146}$': This would give the mass of $\frac{r}{146}$ objects, the opposite of what is needed. ✗
  • Choice D — '$146t$': Multiplying $146r$ objects by $\frac{t}{r}$ each correctly gives $146t$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
